nodes.label: main.py
nodes.role: Serves as the FastAPI application entry point that initializes the memory system, configures CORS middleware, and manages application lifecycle events.
nodes.plain: This is the main startup file that gets everything running - it starts the web server, sets up the memory system that remembers conversations, and makes sure all the different parts of the app can talk to each other.
notes: line:28, initializes global memory system reference
line:35, implements FastAPI lifespan context manager for startup/shutdown
line:42, attempts to initialize memory system with data storage path
line:45, runs memory system health tests on startup

nodes.label: code_agent.py
nodes.role: AI agent that generates Streamlit application code using Google Gemini as primary model with provisions for a fine-tuned QWEN 2.5 fallback model.
nodes.plain: This is like having a coding assistant that can write entire apps for you - it takes your app idea and automatically generates the actual code files, with a backup system in case the main AI service is down.
notes: line:24, initializes Google Gemini model with specific temperature settings
line:35, includes commented-out fine-tuned model initialization for fallback
line:42, sets finetuned_available flag to false when model is disabled
line:44, contains extensive commented code for local model integration

nodes.label: email_agent.py
nodes.role: AI agent that generates marketing emails and launch campaigns using Gemini Pro and integrates with Gmail through the EmailSender component.
nodes.plain: This creates marketing emails for your apps automatically - when you build something, it can write professional launch announcements and promotional emails, then actually send them through Gmail.
notes: line:17, initializes Gemini model with 0.4 temperature for creative email content
line:25, creates EmailSender instance for Gmail integration
line:31, defines generate_launch_email method with configurable audience and email type
line:40, calls helper methods to generate subject lines and email body content

nodes.label: email_sender.py
nodes.role: Email delivery service that sends emails through Gmail using MCP (Model Context Protocol) client with automatic token refresh capabilities.
nodes.plain: This is the part that actually sends emails - it connects to Gmail and handles all the technical details of email delivery, including automatically refreshing security tokens so you don't get logged out.
notes: line:16, checks for npx availability and initializes MCP Gmail client
line:20, provides status messaging about automatic token refresh feature
line:30, defines send_email method with recipient validation
line:35, includes email data validation before attempting to send

nodes.label: leonardo_agent.py
nodes.role: AI image generation agent that creates marketing images using Google's generative AI with workflow-aware prompt engineering.
nodes.plain: This creates custom images for your apps - like logos, marketing graphics, or promotional images - by understanding what your app does and generating appropriate visuals automatically.
notes: line:18, initializes Google Generative AI client for image generation
line:21, sets up Gemini LLM for intelligent prompt generation
line:32, defines generate_marketing_image method with app context integration
line:37, extracts app metadata for context-aware image generation

nodes.label: memory_retrieval_agent.py
nodes.role: Simplified memory agent that retrieves relevant context from past conversations using semantic search without regex pattern matching.
nodes.plain: This helps the system remember previous conversations and find relevant information when you ask questions - it's like giving the AI a memory of what you've discussed before.
notes: line:17, initializes with low temperature (0.2) for consistent responses
line:23, removes regex-based pattern matching in favor of semantic search
line:29, implements get_relevant_context method with configurable story limits
line:35, uses vector memory manager for semantic story search

nodes.label: router_agent.py
nodes.role: AI-driven routing agent that analyzes user intents and determines which application or service should handle specific requests using Gemini Pro.
nodes.plain: This is like a smart receptionist that figures out what you want to do and directs you to the right part of the system - whether you want to create an app, send emails, or generate images.
notes: line:21, defines AppType enum with available applications in the ecosystem
line:32, initializes Gemini model with 0.3 temperature for natural routing decisions
line:43, includes minimal hardcoded app descriptions for AI context
line:17, marked as dead code but contains critical routing logic

nodes.label: story_writer_agent.py
nodes.role: Converts user actions and app interactions into narrative user stories for semantic search and user journey documentation.
nodes.plain: This takes everything you do in the system and writes it down like a story, making it easier to search through your history and understand patterns in how you use different features.
notes: line:16, initializes with 0.4 temperature for creative storytelling
line:23, implements write_story method to convert events into narratives
line:26, accepts event_data with app_name, action, timestamp, and user context
line:37, returns structured story format for semantic search optimization

nodes.label: __init__.py
nodes.role: Empty initialization file that makes the api directory a Python package.
nodes.plain: This is just a placeholder file that tells Python this folder contains code that other parts of the system can use - it's completely empty but necessary for the system to work.
notes: line:1, contains no code - empty file
line:1, serves as Python package marker for api directory

nodes.label: routes.py
nodes.role: FastAPI router that defines all API endpoints, manages session storage, and coordinates between different agents for chat and app generation workflows.
nodes.plain: This handles all the web requests that come into the system - when you send a message or request an action through the web interface, this file figures out what to do and coordinates all the different AI agents.
notes: line:58, implements _extract_app_name_from_text function with regex patterns for app name detection
line:87, defines _extract_app_category_from_text with keyword-based category classification
line:109, includes _ensure_session_structure for session management
line:17, initializes all agent instances (router, leonardo, code, email agents)

nodes.label: gmail_mcp_client.py
nodes.role: Manages Gmail integration through the Model Context Protocol (MCP) with automatic OAuth token refresh using a dedicated thread pool for asynchronous operations.
nodes.plain: This handles connecting to Gmail so the app can read and send emails automatically. It takes care of staying logged in without asking users to manually sign in through their browser every time.
notes: line:21, initializes dedicated thread pool for MCP operations
line:29, configures MCP server using npx and headless Gmail package
line:16, sets up automatic token refresh functionality

nodes.label: memory_system.py
nodes.role: Orchestrates the complete user story memory system by coordinating story writing, vector storage, and retrieval components.
nodes.plain: This is like the brain's memory center - it remembers what users have done before (like creating apps or sending emails) and stores those experiences as stories so the AI assistant can learn from past interactions.
notes: line:15, initializes vector manager, story writer, and memory retriever components
line:26, records app actions as user stories with session context
line:256, provides global singleton access to memory system
line:265, handles async initialization with custom storage path

nodes.label: vector_memory_manager.py
nodes.role: Handles vector embedding, FAISS-based storage, and semantic retrieval of user stories using Google's embedding API.
nodes.plain: This converts user stories into mathematical representations that the computer can search through quickly, like turning written memories into a filing system the AI can instantly look through to find similar past experiences.
notes: line:23, initializes FAISS vector storage with 768-dimension Google embeddings
line:31, sets up file paths for index, metadata, and story text storage
line:35, configures Google AI API for text embeddings
line:38, defines maximum capacity of 10,000 stored stories

nodes.label: logger.py
nodes.role: Provides centralized logging configuration with file and console output using Python's built-in logging framework.
nodes.plain: This keeps track of what the app is doing by writing down important events and errors in log files, like keeping a diary of the app's activities for debugging when something goes wrong.
notes: line:11, creates daily log files with timestamp naming
line:15, configures both file and console logging output
line:28, provides factory function to get named loggers for different modules

nodes.label: qwen_streamlit_coder.py
nodes.role: Contains commented-out Google Colab notebook code for fine-tuning Qwen language model with synthetic data generation capabilities.
nodes.plain: This is training code for customizing an AI model to be better at writing code, but it's currently disabled. It would teach the AI to generate better Streamlit applications by showing it lots of examples.
notes: line:8, contains setup for Hugging Face transformers and model training
line:15, includes HuggingFace login authentication for model access
line:32, checks for GPU availability for training acceleration
line:38, has commented Gemini API setup for synthetic data generation

nodes.label: script.js
nodes.role: Manages the web frontend interface including tab navigation, chat functionality, and direct app generation with file download capabilities.
nodes.plain: This handles the website's user interface - switching between different tools (like email, image creation, app building), managing the chat with the AI assistant, and letting users download the files they create.
notes: line:10, manages tab switching with name mapping for consistent navigation
line:43, handles chat message sending with loading states
line:83, creates and displays chat messages in the interface
line:104, provides direct app generation functionality bypassing chat

executiveSummary.headline: Python-based API service with vector memory management capabilities for data storage and retrieval
executiveSummary.architecture: The system follows a modular architecture with a main entry point orchestrating API routes and a sophisticated memory subsystem. Core functionality is organized into distinct layers: API routing, memory management with vector storage, and service components, indicating a well-structured separation of concerns.
strengths: Clean modular architecture with clear separation between API, memory, and service layers
Implements advanced vector memory management suggesting ML/AI capabilities
concerns: Contains dead code across 5 items indicating potential maintenance issues
Relatively small codebase (18 files, 16 nodes) may suggest incomplete implementation or early development stage
